div1.entryFree:
Stagger, 1) tr. a) to make to reel, to fell down: that hand shall burn in never-quenching fire that --s thus my person, R2 V, 5, 110.
b) to cause to hesitate, to bewilder: the question did at first so s. me, H8 II, 4, 212.
2) intr. to waver, to hesitate: without any pause or --ing take this basket, Wiv. III, 3, 12. whether the tyranny be in his place, or in his eminence, I s. in, Meas. I, 2, 169. a man may s. in this attempt, As III, 3, 49.
